wikitext:
{{Relic Infobox|Pael's Growth|2}}{{Keyword Infobox|Clone|2}}{{R|Pael's Growth||2}} is an {{QueryLink|Relics|rarity:Ancient|Ancient|2}} {{2|Relics|Relic}}. Upon pickup, you [[Slay the Spire 2:Enchantments|Enchant]] a card with {{KW|Clone||2}}, allowing you to duplicate ''every'' copy of it at [[Slay the Spire 2:Rest Sites|Rest Sites.]]

== Sources ==
Can be offered at the start of [[StS2:Acts#Act 2|Act 2]] by {{KW|Pael||2}}.

== Strategy ==
Duplicating a card that is key to your game plan can be very advantageous, but also runs the risk of diluting your deck and preventing you from drawing cards that are important in specific moments. Be sure that whatever card has Clone is strong enough on its own to be worth potentially not drawing other cards, especially if the plan is to create dozens of copies over the course of the run.

If possible, it is best to add Clone to a card which is already upgraded. Upgrading a card before duplicating it means that all copies will have the upgrade, making it more impactful than a single upgrade would otherwise be. Upgrading after taking Pael's Growth means losing out on a chance to duplicate the Clone card.

=== Synergies ===
* Having {{R|Miniature Tent||2}} lets you duplicate cards while also healing and upgrading a card, which also makes it more effective to add Clone to a card which has not yet been upgraded.
* Cards which deal more damage for each card with a certain attribute (such as {{C|Perfected Strike||2}}, {{C|Crescent Spear||2}}, and {{C|Squeeze||2}}) or enhance others copies of themselves (such as {{C|Claw||2}}) are exceptionally powerful if duplicated numerous times.
* Cards like {{C|Flash of Steel||2}} or {{C|Adrenaline||2}}, which cost no {{Icon|CE|2}} and draw a card to replace themselves, are generally safe to clone, as there is usually no downside to having more of them in your deck. There are some exceptions, such as the battles against {{M|Hunter Killer||2}} or {{M|Entomancer||2}}, which can harm the player for each card played.

== Bugs ==
<!-- Include any relevant bugs here. Otherwise delete this section entirely. -->

== Notes ==
* Cloning at a {{2|Rest Site}} will duplicate every card with {{KW|Clone||2}} at once.
* The copies will also have Clone, meaning that each time you use Clone, the number of copies in your deck doubles.
